input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

```powershell
# Needed for WinUI/Windows App SDK support
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

During install (or via `vsconfig`), ensure these components are selected:
- `.NET Multi-platform App UI development`
- `Windows App SDK` (includes WinUI 3)

## 4. Install Android SDK (without Android Studio)

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (required by Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Install Android command-line tools
$cmdlineToolsUrl = "https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only"
# Or directly:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# Set environment variables
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
```

## 5. Accept Licenses & Install Android Platforms

```powershell
# Discover available platforms/build-tools dynamically
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components (adjust versions from --list output)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platform-tools" `
    "platforms;android-34" `
    "build-tools;34.0.0" `
    "emulator" `
    "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 6. (Optional) Create Android Emulator

```powershell
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
    -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_6"
```

## 7. Enable Windows Developer Mode

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 8. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Check MAUI requirements
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test project targeting both platforms
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## Key Tips

| Topic | Command |
|-------|---------|
| Discover .NET versions | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| Find MAUI templates | `dotnet new list maui` |
| Update workloads | `dotnet workload update` |
| List Android targets | `sdkmanager.bat --list` |
| Check Java | `java --version` |

## Dynamic Version Discovery Summary

- **MAUI workload name**: `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Android API levels**: `sdkmanager.bat --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"`
- **Build-tools versions**: `sdkmanager.bat --list | Select-String "build-tools;"`
- **Windows TFM**: check `dotnet new maui --dry-run` output or [MS docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/)

Adjust `net9.0-*` to whatever `dotnet --version` reports (e.g., `net10.0-*` if on .NET 10).
